problem_statement: Menu bar opens correctly (tray icon visible, dropdown works) but the global hotkey (⌥Space by default) does not trigger recording.
note: All issues were identified by cross-referencing specs/, src/, and PRD_3.json. 'checks' are diagnostic steps to confirm the issue. 'status: to do' means unconfirmed at runtime or fix not applied; 'status: done' means code review confirms the item is correctly implemented and no action is needed.

issues.title: Accessibility permission not granted or app binary not in macOS trust list
issues.description: rdev requires Accessibility permission to install a CGEventTap. src/app.rs:423 calls ax_is_process_trusted(); if it returns false, the rdev thread is never spawned and the hotkey is permanently inactive. The menu still works because tray-icon's NSStatusItem does not need Accessibility. Causes: the user dismissed the 'Later' button on the launch alert, the binary path changed since permission was granted (e.g. recompile), or the entry is present but unchecked.
checks: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility; confirm the yaptap binary is listed and its checkbox is checked.
If the binary is absent, drag the binary from target/release/ (or target/debug/) into the list.
If the checkbox was just checked, quit and re-launch (permission takes effect only after restart per spec).

Add a launch-time log: in run_app() before the AXIsProcessTrusted() check, emit `tracing::info!(trusted = crate::hotkey::ax_is_process_trusted(), "accessibility trust")` and confirm the value in Console.app.

issues.title: Input Monitoring permission not granted (macOS 14 Sonoma and later)
issues.description: On macOS 14+, apps must also have Privacy & Security → Input Monitoring permission to receive system-wide keyboard events via CGEventTap. ax_is_process_trusted() only checks Accessibility. If Input Monitoring is absent, CGEventTapCreate may succeed (rdev::listen() starts without error) but the OS silently delivers no events to the tap — the hotkey fires nothing and no error is logged.
checks: Run `sw_vers -productVersion`; if >= 14.0, Input Monitoring applies.
Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Input Monitoring; confirm yaptap is listed and enabled.
If absent: grant it and restart the app.
As a long-term fix, add a runtime check alongside the Accessibility check: query the TCC database or test for a synthetic event and emit a show_alert() if Input Monitoring appears absent.

issues.title: Accessibility permission was granted but the app has not been restarted
issues.description: ax_is_process_trusted() evaluates once at the start of run_app() (src/app.rs:423). If the user grants permission after launch, the rdev thread is not retroactively spawned. The spec (hotkey.md) requires the user to re-launch; the alert message in P3-T049 instructs this. However there is no runtime enforcement — the app silently stays without a hotkey thread.
checks: Confirm the user quit and re-launched after granting permission, not just switched back to the app.
To confirm the rdev thread is alive: add `tracing::info!("rdev thread spawned")` at src/app.rs:438 and check Console.app after launch.

issues.title: rdev::listen() error is only logged to stderr — no UI alert is shown (P3-T052 partial)
issues.description: If rdev::listen() returns an error (e.g. CGEventTapCreate failed due to permission denial or OS rejection), src/app.rs:456–459 calls eprintln! only. The spec (hotkey.md, P3-T052) requires show_alert() with the message 'The hotkey <configured-hotkey> could not be registered. Edit ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml to choose a different hotkey, then restart YapTap.' Without the alert, the user sees a functional menu but a permanently inactive hotkey with no explanation. Note: show_alert() must be called from the main thread; the rdev thread must relay the error via the AppEvent channel rather than calling show_alert() directly.
checks: Open Console.app, filter by process 'yaptap', and look for a 'rdev listen error' message on launch.

Implement the fix: add an AppEvent::HotkeyError(String) variant to the AppEvent enum; in the rdev thread's error branch send AppEvent::HotkeyError(e.to_string()); in the main loop match arm call show_alert() with the spec-required message.
Verify the alert appears when the binary is launched without Accessibility permission.

issues.title: rdev::listen() is called from a std::thread::spawn background thread — CFRunLoop must self-start
issues.description: rdev::listen() on macOS creates a CGEventTap, adds it to CFRunLoopGetCurrent(), and calls CFRunLoopRun(). When invoked from a thread spawned with std::thread::spawn, the thread has its own CFRunLoop which is not running until CFRunLoopRun() is called inside rdev::listen(). This is the correct pattern and should work, but on some rdev versions or macOS releases, the event tap may require the main thread's run loop to be involved.
checks: Check the rdev version in Cargo.lock (`grep -A2 'name = "rdev"' Cargo.lock`) and compare with rdev 0.5 release notes.
Add a heartbeat log in the rdev callback (`tracing::trace!("rdev alive")` on every nth event) to confirm the thread is running and receiving events.
As a smoke test: add a temporary `EventType::MouseMove` listener to the same callback and confirm mouse moves arrive — this rules out the run loop being stuck.

issues.title: rdev 0.5 compatibility with macOS 13–15
issues.description: CGEventTap behaviour has changed across macOS Ventura (13), Sonoma (14), and Sequoia (15). rdev 0.5 predates macOS 15 and may have known incompatibilities. Additionally, unsigned CLI binaries (running from target/release/) are treated differently than .app bundles by the TCC permission system.
checks: Run `sw_vers -productVersion` and search the rdev GitHub issues for the exact macOS version.
Test whether a minimal standalone rdev example (outside yaptap) receives keyboard events on the same machine.
If rdev is the root cause, evaluate upgrading to a newer rdev version or replacing it with a direct CGEventTap implementation using the objc/cocoa crates already in the project.

issues.title: CGEventTap silently receives no events while a secure input field is focused
issues.description: macOS disables HID-level event taps whenever a secure text field (password prompt, sudo in Terminal, 1Password unlock, etc.) is focused. CGEventTapCreate succeeds; rdev::listen() returns no error; but no KeyPress events arrive for the duration of the secure field focus. This is a macOS security feature and cannot be worked around.
checks: Reproduce the failure: confirm no password prompt or secure input field is active in any focused app at the time the hotkey is pressed.
Document this known limitation in a code comment at the rdev thread spawn site (src/app.rs:438).

issues.title: Right Option key maps to rdev::Key::AltGr, not Key::Alt
issues.description: src/hotkey.rs:47–54: parse_modifier("option") returns rdev::Key::Alt, which is hardware keycode 58 (left Option). Hardware keycode 61 (right Option) maps to rdev::Key::AltGr in rdev's macOS backend. If the user presses the right ⌥Option key, the modifier check in src/app.rs:444 fails and HotkeyPressed is never sent.
checks: Test with the LEFT ⌥Option key (left of spacebar) vs the RIGHT ⌥Option key (right of spacebar) — does only one work?

Fix: in parse_modifier(), return a new two-element vec or change the matching logic to treat both Alt and AltGr as satisfying an 'option' modifier. One approach: extend ParsedHotkey.modifiers to a set and add both Key::Alt and Key::AltGr when 'option' is parsed.

issues.title: Key auto-repeat fires multiple HotkeyPressed events per physical press
issues.description: The rdev callback in src/app.rs:441–449 sends AppEvent::HotkeyPressed on every KeyPress event where the hotkey combo is satisfied. macOS sends repeated KeyPress events (~10–30 Hz) while a key is held. For ⌥Space, holding the combo for even 100 ms sends several events, causing Idle→Recording followed immediately by Recording→Processing before the user releases the key. Fix: track a 'hotkey armed' boolean in the rdev closure; set it to false when the main key is pressed (leading edge); reset it to true on KeyRelease of the main key. Fire HotkeyPressed only on the leading edge (armed == true transition to false).
checks: Hold ⌥Space for ~300 ms and observe whether the app transitions directly to Processing without the user releasing the key.
Add a counter to the rdev callback and log how many HotkeyPressed events are sent per physical keypress.

issues.title: HashSet 'sticky key' — Key::Alt may stay in pressed set if its KeyRelease is missed
issues.description: src/app.rs:452–454: rdev::EventType::KeyRelease removes the key from the pressed HashSet. If rdev drops a KeyRelease event (possible under CGEventTap load, when the app switches focus briefly, or during macOS sleep/wake), Key::Alt stays in the set permanently. Consequence: the hotkey then fires on every subsequent Space press alone, without requiring Option to be held. The reverse (Alt release missed so it never re-enters pressed) would silently suppress the hotkey.
checks: Enable tracing in the rdev callback to log every KeyPress/KeyRelease for Key::Alt and Key::Space.
Test: press and release Option repeatedly while watching the logs; confirm no unbalanced insertions accumulate.
Mitigation: clear the pressed HashSet on any event whose type is not KeyPress or KeyRelease (e.g. on mouse click or focus change event if rdev surfaces one), or implement a maximum staleness timer.

issues.title: Hotkey fires when modifier is pressed after main key (out-of-order press)
issues.description: The check at src/app.rs:444–448 fires on every KeyPress event where the full hotkey set is satisfied. If the user presses Space first (no modifier yet — no fire), then presses Option (both now in pressed — fires on the Option KeyPress), the hotkey triggers unexpectedly. This can cause accidental recordings when typing Option+character combinations in other apps if Space happens to be physically held. Fix: change the check to fire only when the key that was just pressed equals parsed.key (the main key), not when a modifier is pressed.
checks: Test: with another app focused, hold Space then press Option — does yaptap start recording?
The fix is a one-line change: replace `if modifiers_held && pressed.contains(&parsed.key)` with `if modifiers_held && key == parsed.key` (using the key variable from the outer KeyPress match arm).

issues.title: AppEvent channel is not drained during show_alert modal — queued hotkey presses execute on dismiss
issues.description: show_alert() calls `msg_send![alert, runModal]` (src/app.rs:118), which blocks the main thread. During this block, app_event_rx.try_recv() (src/app.rs:550) is not called. HotkeyPressed events accumulate in the channel. When the alert is dismissed, all queued events are dispatched in a tight loop, potentially starting a recording immediately after the user clicks OK on an error dialog.
checks: Trigger a pipeline error alert; press ⌥Space while the alert is open; dismiss the alert and observe whether the app immediately starts recording.
Fix: after each runModal call returns, drain app_event_rx with a while-let discard loop before continuing the main event loop.

issues.title: transcription.py and llm.py are referenced by relative paths — fail in app mode
issues.description: src/transcription.rs:28 uses `cmd.arg("src/core/transcribe.py")` and src/llm.rs:33 uses `cmd.arg("src/core/llm.py")`. These paths are relative to the process working directory, which in app mode (launched from Finder, login items, or launchd) is typically `/` or `~`, not the project root. The subprocesses fail to spawn, producing a pipeline error after the first hotkey cycle. The hotkey itself fires correctly, but the pipeline always errors.
checks: Press ⌥Space twice in app mode and observe the error alert. The message will be a spawn error or 'python3: can't open file' — confirm this.

Fix: resolve the script paths relative to the binary using `std::env::current_exe()?.parent()?.join("../src/core/transcribe.py")` (development layout) or embed scripts as resources in a production bundle. Mirror the prompts_dir() logic in config.rs which already handles the binary-relative fallback.

issues.title: AppConfig::load() uses eprintln! for user-facing config errors instead of show_alert() (P3-T017, P3-T018)
issues.description: The spec (config.md, P3-T017/18) requires show_alert() dialogs when the config TOML fails to parse or the hotkey value is invalid. src/config.rs:114–133 uses eprintln! only. In app mode, stderr is invisible to the user. The hotkey silently resets to 'option+space' with no notification. Note: AppConfig::load() is called before NSApplication is fully initialised (before finishLaunching), so show_alert() cannot be called from within load() itself. Fix: return parse/validation warnings alongside the config struct (e.g. a Vec<String> of deferred messages), then show_alert() for each in run_app() after finishLaunching.
checks: Introduce a syntax error in ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml and launch the app. Confirm no alert appears.
Implement the deferred-alert pattern described above and re-test.

issues.title: P3-T069 partial: cpal stream error callback does not send AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err) — app stays in Recording state
issues.description: The spec requires that when the cpal stream error callback fires (audio device lost, driver crash, etc.), the app transitions to Idle and shows an alert. src/audio.rs:127–129 calls tracing::error! only. No AppEvent is sent to the main loop, so the state machine stays in Recording indefinitely. Fix: pass a Sender<AppEvent> into start_recording(); in the error callback send AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err(format!("audio device error: {e}"))).
checks: Simulate a device loss (unplug a USB mic or disable the device in Audio MIDI Setup) while recording.
Confirm the icon does NOT return to Idle and no alert appears — reproducing the bug.
After the fix, repeat the test and confirm the icon returns to Idle with an error alert.

issues.title: P3-T024 task title says NSApplicationActivationPolicyProhibited but correct policy is Accessory — update PRD_3.json
issues.description: The title of P3-T024 in PRD_3.json says 'set the NSApplication activation policy to NSApplicationActivationPolicyProhibited'. The code in src/app.rs:395 correctly uses policy 1 (Accessory), as the comment explains: Prohibited (2) prevents all UI event processing and would break the menu. The implementation is correct per menubar.md. The PRD_3 task title is misleading and should be corrected to prevent future confusion.
checks: Edit PRD_3.json P3-T024 title to: 'In run_app(): set the NSApplication activation policy to NSApplicationActivationPolicyAccessory (1) via the cocoa crate so no Dock icon appears but menu-bar clicks are still processed'.
Confirm the app still launches with no Dock icon and a working menu after the documentation-only change.

issues.title: P3-T006 incomplete: CLI audio path in main.rs still uses inline cpal code instead of audio::start_recording()
issues.description: P3-T006 (status 'to do' in PRD_3.json) requires the CLI path in main.rs to call audio::start_recording() / stop_and_save() from src/audio.rs. main.rs currently contains its own duplicate inline cpal stream setup (~lines 200–484). Any bug fixed in audio.rs is not reflected in CLI mode, and the two implementations can diverge silently.
checks: Confirm main.rs contains `device.build_input_stream` (it does, around line 310) — duplicate of audio.rs.

Refactor main.rs CLI path: replace the inline capture block with `let handle = audio::start_recording(args.device)?;`, block on stdin Enter via read_line, then `let wav_path = handle.stop_and_save()?;`.

issues.title: Tray icon not marked as a macOS template image — may not invert correctly on dark menu bar
issues.description: The spec (menubar.md) requires the PNG icons to be template images so the system inverts them automatically for dark and light menu bar themes. tray_icon::Icon::from_rgba() (src/app.rs:183) creates a raw RGBA icon; it does not set the NSImage templateImage flag. Without the flag, the icon always renders its literal bitmap pixels — a dark icon on a dark menu bar becomes invisible.
checks: Switch the macOS menu bar to dark mode (System Settings → Appearance → Dark) and observe the yaptap icon.
If invisible or wrong colour, retrieve the NSStatusItem's NSImage after building the TrayIcon (via unsafe objc) and call `setTemplate:YES` on it.
Alternatively, check whether the tray-icon 0.14 API exposes a template-image flag and use it.

issues.title: Option+Space may conflict with a system or third-party shortcut consuming the event upstream
issues.description: The spec notes that rdev's CGEventTap is passive (does not suppress the event). However, if another app installs a consuming CGEventTap at the same or higher tap level before rdev, it may intercept and suppress Option+Space before rdev's tap sees it. Common sources: Alfred, Raycast, or System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts assignments for ⌥Space.
checks: Open System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts; search for any ⌥Space binding.
Temporarily quit Alfred / Raycast / any launcher app and test the hotkey.
Test with an alternative hotkey (e.g. cmd+shift+y set in ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml) to rule out a key-specific conflict.

issues.title: No visual feedback between first hotkey press and recording start — hard to diagnose silent failures
issues.description: Between the first ⌥Space press and the icon switching to active, there is no immediate feedback to confirm the hotkey was received. If start_recording() fails silently or the icon switch fails, the user has no signal that the press was registered. This makes it difficult to distinguish 'hotkey not received' from 'hotkey received but recording failed'.
checks: Press ⌥Space once and observe whether the icon changes to the active (three equal bars) variant within ~100 ms.
If the icon never changes, the problem is upstream (hotkey not delivered to the main loop); if it changes but returns to idle quickly, the pipeline is failing.
Add `tracing::info!("hotkey received, state = {:?}", state)` at src/app.rs:552 to make hotkey delivery visible in Console.app without needing a UI change.

issues.title: rdev callback closure must never call Cocoa APIs — no SAFETY comment documents this constraint
issues.description: The rdev callback in src/app.rs:441–454 correctly sends events over an mpsc channel rather than calling show_alert() or any other Cocoa API. However, the constraint is undocumented. A future maintainer adding diagnostic logic inside the callback might inadvertently call msg_send! or show_alert(), which would crash because NSAlert and other AppKit calls must run on the main thread. Per rust-safety.mdc, unsafe contracts must be documented at the site of the constraint.
checks: Confirm no show_alert() or msg_send! calls exist inside the rdev::listen() callback — they do not (correct).

Add a code comment above the callback closure: `// SAFETY: This closure runs on the rdev CGEventTap thread. No Cocoa/AppKit calls are permitted here. All UI feedback must go via the AppEvent channel.`

issues.title: eprintln!/println! used for internal diagnostics — violates rust-tracing.mdc
issues.description: rust-tracing.mdc rule: 'Never use println! or eprintln! for observability.' In app mode, stderr is not visible to the user (Console.app requires manual filtering). Internal diagnostic messages must use the tracing crate so they are controlled by RUST_LOG and visible in structured log output. Affected sites: src/config.rs has ~8 eprintln! calls for config warnings; src/app.rs:457 uses eprintln! for the rdev error. Note: user-facing messages (alerts) correctly use show_alert() — the concern is internal-only diagnostic output.
checks: Search for eprintln!/println! in src/: `grep -rn 'eprintln!\|println!' src/`.
Replace each diagnostic eprintln! with the appropriate tracing level: config warnings → `tracing::warn!`, rdev errors → `tracing::error!`, using structured fields not interpolated strings.
Keep any intentional user-facing stdout output in CLI mode (e.g. 'Recording...', 'Transcribing...') as-is — those are CLI UI output, not observability.

issues.title: unsafe blocks in app.rs lack // SAFETY: comments — violates rust-safety.mdc
issues.description: rust-safety.mdc rule: 'Every unsafe block must have a comment explaining the invariant that makes it sound.' src/app.rs contains multiple bare unsafe blocks without SAFETY justifications: show_alert() (~lines 103–120, NSAlert/NSString FFI), run_app() (~lines 393–399, NSApplication setup), the main event loop (~lines 492–512, nextEventMatchingMask/sendEvent/updateWindows), and the autorelease pool drain (~line 616). The unsafe impl Send/Sync for SharedState (lines 70–71) has a comment but it conflates Send and Sync without explaining why Sync is also valid.
checks: Run `grep -n 'unsafe' src/app.rs` to list all unsafe sites.

Add a `// SAFETY:` comment above each unsafe block explaining: (a) why the Cocoa object pointers are valid, (b) that msg_send! is only called from the main thread, (c) why autorelease pool drain is sound.

Expand the SharedState Sync justification: 'SAFETY: SharedState is Sync because all fields are Arc<Mutex<T>>, which are Sync. The unsafe impl is required because AudioHandle contains cpal::Stream which is !Send on macOS; access is always mediated by the Mutex guard.'

issues.title: #[allow(clippy::comparison_to_empty)] suppresses a fixable lint — violates rust-style.mdc
issues.description: rust-style.mdc rule: 'Run clippy with cargo clippy -- -D warnings; every warning is a bug.' src/app.rs uses `#[allow(clippy::comparison_to_empty)]` at two sites (lines 279 and 370) to suppress a warning on `config.selected_prompt == ""`. The correct fix is `config.selected_prompt.is_empty()`, which is more idiomatic and removes the need for the allow attribute. A separate `#[allow(clippy::arc_with_non_send_sync)]` on SharedState::new() is present but may be necessary given the !Send inner type; it should be accompanied by a comment explaining why.
checks: Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm these lints are the only suppressed ones.
Replace `config.selected_prompt == ""` with `config.selected_prompt.is_empty()` in both locations.
For the arc_with_non_send_sync allow: add a comment explaining it is suppressed because AudioHandle contains cpal::Stream which is !Send by cpal's design, and access is guarded by a Mutex.

issues.title: let _ = menu.append(...) silently discards errors in build_menu() — violates rust-errors.mdc
issues.description: rust-errors.mdc rule: 'BAD — silently discards errors: `let _ = do_thing();`'. src/app.rs build_menu() (~lines 276–303) uses `let _ = menu.append(...)` for every menu item. If tray-icon returns an error for any append (e.g. menu item limit exceeded, invalid state), the item is silently omitted from the menu with no logging. Fix: replace with `if let Err(e) = menu.append(...) { tracing::warn!(error = ?e, "failed to append menu item"); }`.
checks: Run `grep -n 'let _ = menu.append' src/app.rs` to confirm all affected lines.
Replace each with the tracing::warn! pattern above.

issues.title: P3-T033 partial: @2x Retina icon constants not defined in app.rs
issues.description: P3-T033 requires all four icon PNGs to be embedded at compile time as constants ICON_IDLE_1X, ICON_IDLE_2X, ICON_ACTIVE_1X, ICON_ACTIVE_2X. src/app.rs:24–25 defines only ICON_IDLE_1X and ICON_ACTIVE_1X. ICON_IDLE_2X and ICON_ACTIVE_2X are never defined or used. The @2x icons therefore never load, meaning Retina displays always render the 1x icon (blurry on high-DPI screens). Fix: add `const ICON_IDLE_2X: &[u8] = include_bytes!("../assets/icons/yaptap-idle@2x.png");` and the active equivalent, then pass both to the TrayIcon builder if tray-icon supports Retina variants.
checks: Confirm assets/icons/yaptap-idle@2x.png and yaptap-active@2x.png exist: `ls assets/icons/`.
Add the two missing include_bytes! constants in src/app.rs.
Check tray-icon 0.14 API for a Retina / @2x icon method; use it or document that tray-icon does not yet support Retina overrides.
